title: Notational Intelligence, Linus Lee | Compile 26
summary: Linus Lee argues that notational intelligence, the way we write and represent ideas, fundamentally shapes our thinking and cognition. He proposes that deep learning models could potentially invent entirely new mathematical and symbolic notations that humans have not yet conceived, expanding our ability to reason about complex domains.
writeup: In this Compile 26 talk, Linus Lee makes the case that notational intelligence, the way ideas get written down and represented, shapes thinking and cognition rather than merely recording it. The argument extends to deep learning: models could invent entirely new mathematical and symbolic notations that humans have not yet conceived, expanding the capacity to reason about complex domains. The throughline connects representation and abstraction to intelligence itself, framing notation as an active driver of how problems can be reasoned about.
